channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Qualified Health Raises $125M for Hospital AI Platform
item.description: Qualified Health closed a $125M Series B led by NEA, valuing the startup at up to $1 billion. Its platform supports 500,000 users across 16 health systems. At UTMB, one deployment delivered $15 million in run-rate impact in six months. Anthropic invested as b… [Anthropic]

item.title: Semicon China 2026: AI spending nears US$800 billion, IBS flags 2028 chip cycle risk
item.description: SEMICON China 2026 spotlighted the scale of the AI investment boom, with Handel Jones, CEO of International Business Strategies (IBS), estimating that global AI and data center capital expenditure has surged from about US$110 billion in 2020 to roughly US$600…

item.title: South Korean chip giants step up China investments to combat global AI memory shortage
item.description: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are stepping up investments in their China wafer fabs as they race to boost supply amid a tightening memory chip market driven by demand for artificial intelligence computing, underscoring China’s continued role in semiconduct… [Samsung, Intel]

item.title: Elon Musk warns the U.S. could soon be producing more chips than we can turn on. And China doesn’t have the same issue
item.description: China has rapidly expanded production capacity thanks to a reliance on solar energy, and Tesla is taking a page from its playbook. [Tesla]

item.title: Bittensor ecosystem tokens' value hit $1.5 billion as Jensen Huang endorsement supports TAO rally
item.description: The ecosystem's smaller tokens are acting as leveraged bets on TAO, with multiple subnet tokens posting 200-400% monthly gains.

item.title: Recap: Europe’s top funding rounds this week (23–29 March)
item.description: A week that spanned semiconductor physics, orbital logistics, defence interceptors, and carob-based chocolate tells you something about the breadth of European and European adjacent capital right now. The dominant thread is not a single sector but a single in… [ServiceNow]

item.title: The AI doomsday everyone’s worried about is the wrong one
item.description: Companies are spending 93% of their AI budgets on tech and only 7% on people. It's already backfiring.

item.title: Cash In This Weekend With Over 100 Tech Deals From Apple, LG, Samsung, and More at Amazon's Big Spring Sale
item.description: Yes, we're working all weekend, but it's for a good reason: bringing you the deepest discounts on top-rated electronics during Amazon's massive Big Spring Sale. We've hit the halfway mark for Amazon's Big Spring Sale, but we're not slowing down until we've br… [Amazon, Samsung, Apple]

item.title: All 11 xAI co-founders have now reportedly left Elon Musk’s AI company
item.description: Every co-founder Elon Musk recruited to build xAI has now reportedly left the company. Manuel Kroiss, who led the pretraining team, told people this month that he was departing. Ross Nordeen, described by Business Insider as Musk’s “right-hand operator,” left… [ServiceNow]

item.title: Jeff Bezos Pushes Blue Origin Into Orbital Data Centers With Massive Satellite Plan
item.description: Jeff Bezos, the billionaire founder of Amazon and owner of The Washington Post, made headlines in March 2026 when his space company Blue Origin filed ambitious plans with federal regulators for "Project Sunrise," a proposed network of up to 51,600 satellites … [Amazon]

item.title: Crypto's future is bright in the context of AI's assault on software firms, says Kraken-backed investment firm
item.description: Crypto’s latest bear cycle is a mere blip when compared with the existential threat AI now poses to traditional software services, says Ravi Tanuku, CEO of KRAKacquisition Corp. [ServiceNow]

item.title: The crypto winners from AI may not be AI coins at all as agents start spending autonomously
item.description: AI agents are moving beyond chatbot duty and into a bigger role across the internet. As software starts researching, buying, coordinating, and completing tasks with limited supervision, a new question arises: how does a non-human user pay, prove who it is, an…

item.title: Memory stocks have tanked, but BofA says the sharp sell-off is overdone
item.description: Memory stocks like Micron sold off after Google unveiled its new compression tech, but Bank of America says investor fears are overblown. [Alphabet]
